notes: The SDO Observatory record itself lists only one Contact (W. Dean Pesnell, ProjectScientist) and contains NO InstrumentID links. The three instruments (AIA, EVE, HMI) were located by their conventional SPASE Instrument IDs (spase://SMWG/Instrument/SDO/{AIA,EVE,HMI}) and confirmed via the provider page; consider adding these InstrumentIDs to the Observatory record downstream.

Instrument PIs are included as candidates because they lead SDO's three science investigations; the downstream role-assignment agent should decide whether instrument-level PIs belong on the Observatory record vs. only on their respective Instrument records.

The AIA Instrument record distinguishes a current PI (Meng Jin) from three FormerPIs (Cheung, Schrijver, Title) — all carried forward as candidates; activity-window/role ordering is left to the downstream agent.
EVE and HMI Instrument records each list a single PI (Woods, Scherrer respectively); no FormerPI entries were present.

Mission description paper used: 'The Solar Dynamics Observatory (SDO)', Pesnell, Thompson & Chamberlin, Solar Physics 2012, DOI 10.1007/s11207-011-9841-3 — matches the operating-span start (launch 11 Feb 2010) within the expected 2–5 year window. Used only for lead-author/PI selection, not for dumping co-authors.
No RevisionHistory or PublicationInfo was present in the Observatory record to evaluate metadata submitters/curators.
Phillip C. Chamberlin has no SMWG Person record (404 at the expected First.Last URL); name spelling 'Phillip' vs 'Phillip C. Chamberlin' should be verified downstream if he is ever promoted.
